title: Export Project

project.title: Export Project (.tix)
project.description: To continue working later or share with your team

training.title: Export for Training
training.description: Dataset ready to train your model (always includes images)

template.importantCLI: IMPORTANT: YOLOv5 uses CLI, not Python API
template.importantDetectron2: IMPORTANT: Detectron2 is more advanced but complex
template.importantSegmentation: IMPORTANT: Semantic segmentation with modern architectures
template.registerCOCO: Register your dataset in COCO format
template.finalLearningRate: Final learning rate (as fraction of lr0)
template.valPercentage: Validation percentage if no val split exists
template.executeCmds: Run these commands in your terminal
template.yoloUsesCli: YOLOv5 uses CLI, not Python API

tooltips.framework: Deep learning library to use. Adapts according to your project type.
tooltips.model: Model size: Nano is fast but less accurate, XLarge is slow but more precise.
tooltips.device: Where to train: CPU is slow but always works, GPU (CUDA) is very fast if you have NVIDIA.
tooltips.epochs: How many times the model sees the entire dataset. More epochs = more learning but more time.
tooltips.batch: How many images to process at once. Higher values use more memory but train faster.
tooltips.imgsz: Resolution of images during training. Larger = more detail but slower.
tooltips.seqLength: Number of time steps to use as input. E.g., use 50 past values to predict the future.
tooltips.forecastHorizon: How many future steps to predict (forecasting only). E.g., predict next 10 values.
tooltips.hiddenSize: Size of the neural network hidden layer (LSTM). Larger = more capacity but slower.
tooltips.optimizer: Algorithm that adjusts model weights during training. Adam: balanced and popular. AdamW: Adam with improved weight decay. SGD: classic, slower but sometimes better final result. RMSprop: good for RNNs.
tooltips.lr: How fast the model learns. High values = learns fast but can be unstable. 0.001 is a good starting point.
tooltips.patience: How many epochs to wait without improvement before stopping. If model doesn't improve in 50 epochs, stops automatically.
tooltips.valSplit: What percentage of your images to use for validation. 20% is standard: 80% trains, 20% validates.
tooltips.augmentationTitle: Techniques to create variations of your images and prevent the model from memorizing. Helps generalize better.
tooltips.augMosaic: Combines 4 random images into one. Very effective for improving small object detection.
tooltips.augMixup: Mixes two images with transparency. Helps model be more robust against occlusions.
tooltips.augHsv: Changes image colors (hue, saturation, brightness). Useful for different lighting conditions.
tooltips.augFlip: Flips images horizontally or vertically. Doubles your data effortlessly.
tooltips.augRotate: Rotates images slightly. Useful if your objects can appear at different angles.
tooltips.augScale: Zooms in/out and randomly crops. Simulates objects at different distances.
tooltips.metricsTitle: What information to save during training to analyze model performance.
tooltips.savePlots: Saves loss and accuracy plots during training. Very useful to see if the model is learning.
tooltips.saveConfMatrix: Table showing which classes are confused with each other. Ideal for understanding model errors.
tooltips.savePrCurves: Graphs showing the balance between precision and recall. Important for evaluating detection quality.
tooltips.savePredictions: Saves images with model predictions drawn on top. Perfect for visually seeing how well it works.
tooltips.saveMetricsCsv: Exports all metrics to a CSV file for analysis in Excel or creating your own graphs.
tooltips.exportTitle: Formats for deploying your trained model in production.
tooltips.exportOnnx: ONNX: universal format, works with TensorFlow, PyTorch, and almost any library. Ideal for production.
tooltips.exportTorchscript: TorchScript: PyTorch native format, very fast but only works with PyTorch.
tooltips.exportTflite: TensorFlow Lite: for mobile and embedded devices (Android, iOS, Raspberry Pi).
tooltips.exportOpenvino: OpenVINO: optimized for Intel CPUs. Very fast on Intel hardware.
tooltips.exportCoreml: CoreML: for native iOS/macOS apps. Takes advantage of Apple chips.
tooltips.exportTensorrt: TensorRT: maximum acceleration on NVIDIA GPUs. For high-performance production.

formats.yoloDetectionDesc: Bounding boxes in normalized YOLO format (.txt) with data.yaml configuration
formats.yoloSegmentationDesc: Polygons in YOLO segmentation format (.txt) with data.yaml configuration
formats.cocoDesc: COCO JSON format compatible with Mask R-CNN, RetinaNet, and Detectron2
formats.pascalVocDesc: Pascal VOC XML format with pixel coordinates (legacy format)
formats.csvDetectionDesc: CSV tabular format with bounding box coordinates
formats.csvClassificationDesc: CSV tabular format with image filename and class label
formats.csvKeypointsDesc: CSV tabular format with keypoint X, Y coordinates and visibility
formats.csvLandmarksDesc: CSV tabular format with landmark coordinates
formats.foldersByClassDesc: Images organized into separate folders by class name
formats.unetMasksDesc: PNG grayscale mask images for semantic segmentation training
formats.tixDesc: Export the entire project in a .tix file for backup or sharing
formats.huggingfaceAsrDesc: CSV with file_name and transcription columns for speech recognition datasets
formats.ljspeechDesc: LJSpeech format with metadata.csv (name|transcription) and wavs folder
formats.csvAudioClassificationDesc: CSV with file_name and label columns for audio classification
formats.csvSoundEventsDesc: CSV with file_name, start_ms, end_ms and label columns for sound event detection

notAvailable: Export not available for this project type
description: Select one of the available formats to export the project
